title: Yum! Brands (YUM) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Yum! Brands (YUM), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Yum! Brands operates a highly attractive, asset-light franchise model. With immense global scale, strong unit economics, and powerful brand recognition across KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, it boasts a very durable economic moat.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Excellent profitability and global growth driven by an asset-light model.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: While top-line growth is moderate at 6.5%, the company's staggering ~32% operating margins highlight the incredible efficiency of its 98% franchised model.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Yum! continues to aggressively expand its footprint globally, consistently adding thousands of net new units annually, expanding its dominant market share.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Its brands command strong pricing power, though they must balance price increases carefully to maintain value perception among lower-income consumers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Continuous menu innovation, especially at Taco Bell, keeps the brands relevant and drives consistent traffic and same-store sales growth.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Wide moat built on intangible brand assets and massive global scale.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Consumer switching costs in fast food are non-existent; however, franchisee switching costs are relatively high due to long-term contracts and investments.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Massive global scale creates a network effect for marketing, supply chain purchasing power, and technology investments that smaller rivals cannot match.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company's powerful, globally recognized trademarks (KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut) are its most valuable intangible assets.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The highly franchised, asset-light model requires minimal capital expenditure from Yum!, resulting in massive free cash flow generation ($1.3B).

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Stable sentiment with focus on unit growth and digital sales.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Earnings estimates are generally stable, though occasionally impacted by foreign exchange headwinds or macroeconomic weakness in specific international markets like China.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative remains positive, focusing on Yum!'s resilience as a value-oriented option and its aggressive digital transformation efforts.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management expertly utilizes the massive free cash flow to consistently repurchase shares and pay dividends, driving shareholder value.

keyRisks: Macroeconomic pressures and inflation negatively impacting the lower-income consumer demographic.
Significant exposure to the Chinese market (via Yum China) and associated geopolitical or economic risks.
Intense competition in the quick-service restaurant sector globally.
keyCatalysts: Continued acceleration in digital sales and loyalty program adoption driving higher ticket sizes.
Faster-than-expected unit growth in emerging markets like India and Southeast Asia.
Successful turnaround and revitalization of the Pizza Hut brand globally.
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored YUM at 74/100 and Opus at 69/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
